+### Sharpest current abstraction pain point
+
+**The `editor-actions::Action` trait's
+`apply(&self, world: &mut World)` and `revert(&self, world: &mut World)`
+World-only signatures cannot mediate CAD-store (`CadGraph` +

+user-driven editor dispatches.** PLAN §6.16.3's two-layer Command Bus

+specified but unimplemented; the `BusEntry` enum currently ships only the
+`Action(Box<dyn Action>)` variant with `#[non_exhaustive]` marking the
+future slot.

+### Candidate comparison
+
+Five plausible candidates surfaced from current evidence. Each is checked
+against four lenses: (a) is it *current abstraction pressure* (not
+historical state), (b) is it ordinary deferred implementation rather than
+a missing shape, (c) is it doctrine-frozen and therefore an intentional
+cavity, and (d) is it a missing orchestrator-protocol packet type (which
+the AGENTS.md guidance flags as a common false-positive).
+
+| Candidate | Why considered | Why **not** sharpest |
+|---|---|---|
+| **(WINNER) `Action` trait World-only signature blocks CAD-edit dispatches** | The Phase 9 keyboard wire-up just shipped a `SetTimeScale` Action (resource-on-World — fits the trait). The natural next user dispatch (spawn / parametric-edit a B-Rep entity through Ctrl+Z-undoable interaction) requires CadGraph + CadProjection access that `Action::apply(&self, world: &mut World)` cannot supply. The pain is **load-bearing now**, **named in code at `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:492-507`** as an intentional gap, **named in current docs at `plans/BASELINE.md:596-607`**, and corroborated by the PLAN §6.16.3-vs-implementation gap (`BusEntry::CadCheckpoint` paper-only). | n/a — selected as the sharpest. |
+| Editor-usability gap — "substrate-first works but no user-input path connects to a visible scene change" | Recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` `Editor-usability preflight`. | This *was* the sharpest as of the 2026-05-21 preflight, but on this branch the **Phase 9 keyboard → CommandBus wire-up has already shipped** (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:66-135`; six bound key commands at `:122-135`; `SetTimeScale` as the first production `Action` impl at `:193`). The remaining residual pain — the World-only signature — is exactly the WINNER above, which is the *causal substrate* under the editor-usability gap, not a parallel finding. +`CadProjection`) mutations, blocking the natural next class of

+### Candidate comparison
+
+Five plausible candidates surfaced from current evidence. Each is checked
+against four lenses: (a) is it *current abstraction pressure* (not
+historical state), (b) is it ordinary deferred implementation rather than
+a missing shape, (c) is it doctrine-frozen and therefore an intentional
+cavity, and (d) is it a missing orchestrator-protocol packet type (which
+the AGENTS.md guidance flags as a common false-positive).
+
+| Candidate | Why considered | Why **not** sharpest |
+|---|---|---|
+| **(WINNER) `Action` trait World-only signature blocks CAD-edit dispatches** | The Phase 9 keyboard wire-up just shipped a `SetTimeScale` Action (resource-on-World — fits the trait). The natural next user dispatch (spawn / parametric-edit a B-Rep entity through Ctrl+Z-undoable interaction) requires CadGraph + CadProjection access that `Action::apply(&self, world: &mut World)` cannot supply. The pain is **load-bearing now**, **named in code at `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:492-507`** as an intentional gap, **named in current docs at `plans/BASELINE.md:596-607`**, and corroborated by the PLAN §6.16.3-vs-implementation gap (`BusEntry::CadCheckpoint` paper-only). | n/a — selected as the sharpest. |
+| Editor-usability gap — "substrate-first works but no user-input path connects to a visible scene change" | Recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` `Editor-usability preflight`. | This *was* the sharpest as of the 2026-05-21 preflight, but on this branch the **Phase 9 keyboard → CommandBus wire-up has already shipped** (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:66-135`; six bound key commands at `:122-135`; `SetTimeScale` as the first production `Action` impl at `:193`). The remaining residual pain — the World-only signature — is exactly the WINNER above, which is the *causal substrate* under the editor-usability gap, not a parallel finding. Choosing "editor-usability" would be redoing a closed preflight; choosing the World-only trait surface names the actual current architectural cavity. |

Not current pain. |

+### Sharpest current abstraction pain point
+
+**The `editor-actions::Action` trait's
+`apply(&self, world: &mut World)` and `revert(&self, world: &mut World)`
+World-only signatures cannot mediate CAD-store (`CadGraph` +
+`CadProjection`) mutations, blocking the natural next class of
+user-driven editor dispatches.** PLAN §6.16.3's two-layer Command Bus
+model with a `BusEntry::CadCheckpoint { id, description }` variant is
+specified but unimplemented; the `BusEntry` enum currently ships only the
+`Action(Box<dyn Action>)` variant with `#[non_exhaustive]` marking the
+future slot.
+
+Concrete file:line evidence (re-read this dispatch):
+
+- `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:87`:
+  `fn apply(&self, world: &mut World) -> Result<(), ActionResult>;` — the
+  trait method every editor mutation must implement; binds the Action to
+  `rge_kernel_ecs::World` only.
+- `crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:96`:

+### Candidate comparison
+
+Five plausible candidates surfaced from current evidence. Each is checked
+against four lenses: (a) is it *current abstraction pressure* (not
+historical state), (b) is it ordinary deferred implementation rather than
+a missing shape, (c) is it doctrine-frozen and therefore an intentional
+cavity, and (d) is it a missing orchestrator-protocol packet type (which
+the AGENTS.md guidance flags as a common false-positive).
+
+| Candidate | Why considered | Why **not** sharpest |
+|---|---|---|
+| **(WINNER) `Action` trait World-only signature blocks CAD-edit dispatches** | The Phase 9 keyboard wire-up just shipped a `SetTimeScale` Action (resource-on-World — fits the trait). The natural next user dispatch (spawn / parametric-edit a B-Rep entity through Ctrl+Z-undoable interaction) requires CadGraph + CadProjection access that `Action::apply(&self, world: &mut World)` cannot supply. The pain is **load-bearing now**, **named in code at `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:492-507`** as an intentional gap, **named in current docs at `plans/BASELINE.md:596-607`**, and corroborated by the PLAN §6.16.3-vs-implementation gap (`BusEntry::CadCheckpoint` paper-only). | n/a — selected as the sharpest. |
+| Editor-usability gap — "substrate-first works but no user-input path connects to a visible scene change" | Recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` `Editor-usability preflight`. | This *was* the sharpest as of the 2026-05-21 preflight, but on this branch the **Phase 9 keyboard → CommandBus wire-up has already shipped** (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:66-135`; six bound key commands at `:122-135`; `SetTimeScale` struct at `:173` and `impl Action for SetTimeScale` at `:193`; separate production `impl Action for CompoundAction` at `crates/editor-actions/src/compound.rs:51`). The remaining residual pain — the World-only signature — is exactly the WINNER above, which is the *causal substrate* under the editor-usability gap, not a parallel finding. Choosing "editor-usability" would be redoing a closed preflight; choosing the World-only trait surface names the actual current architectural cavity. |
+| Reflection schema is `the architectural root` but has 0 production consumers | Recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` `§1.1 Reflection-scale honesty preflight`. `kernel/types` is 1,151 LoC; 7 reflected types in workspace; 0 production. | This is *deferred implementation*, not abstraction pain: the trait shape and budget look healthy (BUDGET.md PASS), there is no demonstrated friction blocking adoption — the substrate is simply ahead of consumer demand. The Phase 9 §9 "reflection scale" axis is explicitly described as "STRUCTURALLY UNMEASURABLE until production adoption begins". Honesty gap ≠ shape gap. |
+| Live-inspector wiring blocked by absent egui host | Recorded in `plans/BASELINE.md` `Live-inspector wiring preflight`. 4-of-7 components ready; egui host + delivery substrate + spawner wire-up missing. | Already **partly resolved** on this branch: `EditorShell` now carries `egui_host: Option<EguiHost>` at `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:533` and `inspector_handoff: Option<Arc<InspectorHandoff>>` at `:554`. The remaining piece is an ordinary integration dispatch (snapshot-delivery option C per the preflight), not a missing abstraction. |
+| `cad-projection` invalidation density / `kernel/graph-foundation` reverse-dep radius | PLAN §1.10.4 lists `cad-projection invalidation density` and `incremental invalidation radius` as Phase 9 entropy metrics with explicit thresholds. | The 2026-05-21 §1.10.4 preflight measured the workspace and found the highest fanout at **`kernel/graph-foundation` 18.9 % of 95 crates** — ~11.1 pp under the 30 % lint-warn threshold (`change.md:1635` entry — live re-read this dispatch). Not current pain. |
+| Missing orchestrator `_REVIEW_` / `_CLOSEOUT_` packets | Tempting from a file-listing perspective. | Explicitly **not pain** per `AGENTS.md` "Orchestrator dispatch vs. manual protocol" section: an orchestrator dispatch is *expected* to produce only TASK + EXEC (+ optional CORRECT) and store the Codex control review in a gitignored run-dir JSON. Missing `_REVIEW_` / `_CLOSEOUT_` packets are not evidence of an unfinished dispatch. |

+The WINNER is sharper than each of the other four because it is the only
+candidate where (a) the abstraction surface is named and locked in
+**current code** rather than docs, (b) it actively blocks the **next**
+user-facing dispatch under production pressure rather than a forecast
+one, (c) it is **not** doctrine-frozen — PLAN §6.16.3 already commits to
+the two-layer model that resolves it, so this is implementation-pressure
+asking for a planned-but-unimplemented shape, not a §0.6 freeze-policy
+bypass — and (d) it is not a missing protocol packet (those are
+intentional per AGENTS.md).
